project_goal: AIOS — AI-native Linux distribution. Implement the spec contracts from rev.2 (53 sub-specs CONTRACT-grade) into working Rust + Python + KDE code, following §22 MVP golden path discipline.
current_milestone: M2 — Evidence Log skeleton (S3.1 aios-evidence crate)
current_task_title: (queued) — next M2 task TBD by Governor; T-010 segment sealing complete
current_step: T-010 landed; M2 advancing — aios-evidence now carries Segment / SealedSegment / SegmentChain over Ed25519 per S3.1 §5.2 + §7. Segment::seal() consumes the open segment, appends a terminal SEGMENT_SEALED receipt (per-receipt signed by the same key), computes BLAKE3-256 over canonical seal metadata, Ed25519-signs the hex digest, and produces an immutable SealedSegment with cross-segment chain fields. SegmentChain provides append-only verification (verify_chain / verify_chain_signed / verify_full).
completed_steps_M1: T-001: aios-action crate skeleton + 8 prefix-namespaced ID types + 5-phase FSM + 4-section envelope + 11-variant error subset; 10 unit tests; cargo build/test/clippy all pass

T-002: canonical.rs (jcs_canonicalize + blake3_hash + blake3_truncated + CanonicalError); Request::request_hash; ActionEnvelope::idempotency_hash; 14 new tests (10 → 24); no new deps added; ECMA-262 number normalization deferred to T-006 with documented limitation
T-003: extended prefix-namespace registry — 5 ULID-bodied newtypes + content-addressed TransitionPlanId; IdError::InvalidHexBody added; 8 new tests (24 → 32)

T-004: lifecycle FSM behaviour layer — ConditionType closed enum (13 variants); ActionEnvelope::transition_to / add_condition / canonical_conditions_for_phase / validate_phase_conditions; TransitionError enum; 25 new tests (32 → 57)

T-005: full error taxonomy — ActionErrorCode closed enum (35 variants); ActionError struct (code/message/retryable/cause); MAX_CAUSE_CHAIN_DEPTH=8; From<IdError>/From<TransitionError>; 19 new tests (57 → 76)

T-006: serde_jcs 0.1 adopted to workspace deps (Governor approved) — canonical.rs now delegates to RFC 8785 implementation; ECMA-262 number normalization deficit from T-002 is CLOSED (verified by golden test: numerically-equal f64 values 150.0 and 1.5e2 produce identical canonical bytes + identical request_hash); CanonicalError simplified to single Projection variant; tests/ integration target with common/mod.rs shared helpers + 3 test files (golden_envelope.rs: 8 tests, lifecycle_replay.rs: 6 tests, error_paths.rs: 12 tests = 26 integration tests); 3 hand-written golden JSON fixtures under tests/fixtures/ (envelope_success_full / envelope_policy_denied / envelope_rolled_back) — each parses, validates phase ↔ conditions, and round-trips through JCS canonical bytes lossless; M1 closed at 76 unit + 26 integration = 102 tests
completed_steps_M2: T-007: aios-evidence crate skeleton — Cargo.toml registers crate as workspace member; first cross-crate consumer of aios-action (uses ActionId, EvidenceReceiptId, jcs_canonicalize, blake3_hash, blake3_truncated, IdError, CanonicalError). 5 modules: error / record / receipt / chain / sealed. EvidenceError closed enum with 8 variants (EncodingFailed, HashMismatch, ChainBroken, GenesisMissing, EmptyChain, DuplicateGenesis, InvalidReceiptId, InvalidSubject). RetentionClass closed 3-variant enum (Standard24M/Extended60M/Forever) with exact SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE serde renames. RecordType closed 39-variant subset of Wave-13 vocabulary (action lifecycle 11, namespace integration 2, vault/recovery/tamper 7, AI subject barriers 4, build/test/release 7, FSM extras 8 — covers everything the M1 aios-action lifecycle would emit), with TODO(T-008) comment marking the path to full 428 entries. EvidenceReceipt struct: all 10 fields private, only &self accessors (receipt_id, recorded_at, record_type, retention_class, subject_canonical_id, action_id, previous_receipt_hash, content_hash, payload, signature). ReceiptBuilder consuming builder pattern with seal() that mints evr_<ULID>, stamps Utc::now(), computes content_hash = BLAKE3-256(JCS(payload)) (64 hex), links previous via prior.link_hash() = BLAKE3-truncated-32(JCS(prior receipt)). ReceiptChain append-only Vec<EvidenceReceipt> with append() validating genesis discipline + link match, verify_integrity() walks windows(2) and recomputes every link. Sealed marker trait + private SealedSuper super-trait makes INV-005 (append-only) enforceable at the type level — impossible for downstream crates to implement Sealed for non-receipt types. 29 unit tests + 2 integration tests (lifecycle_chain.rs: full_action_lifecycle_emits_a_verifiable_chain + chain_serde_round_trips_and_still_verifies) = +31 tests (102 → 133). cargo build/test/clippy --all-targets -D warnings + cargo fmt --check all PASS.

T-008: RecordType expanded to full 427-entry Wave-13 vocabulary (commit 9185413). strum + strum_macros 0.26 added for EnumIter + EnumCount derives. Declaration order matches S3.1 Appendix A IDs 1..427. 158 tests green (+25 over T-007).

T-009: Ed25519 signature path landed (commit 6ee436d) per S3.1 §5.2 / §11.3 / §28.5. Added ed25519-dalek 2.1 (BSD-3-Clause, RustCrypto-adjacent) + rand_core 0.6 to workspace deps. ReceiptBuilder::seal_signed(previous, &SigningKey) shares its core with seal() via private seal_inner. EvidenceReceipt gains verify_signature(&VerifyingKey), is_signed(), signing_digest() (BLAKE3-256 hex of JCS of receipt-minus-signature). Private ReceiptForSigning<'a> serde view omits signature field to avoid sign-the-signature cycle. encode/decode_signature_hex enforce 128-char lowercase hex (uppercase rejected). ReceiptChain::verify_integrity_signed(&VerifyingKey) walks link hashes then verifies every receipt's signature. 3 new EvidenceError variants: SignatureMalformed (hex parse / length), SignatureMissing (verify on unsigned), SignatureMismatch (Ed25519 reject — maps to RECEIPT_FORGERY_DETECTED §28.5). Production key path documented as deferred to S5.2 Vault Broker for subject _system:service:evidence-segment-signer; the &SigningKey parameter will be replaced with a &VaultSignCapability when S5.2 lands. The hash-then-sign envelope (sign the BLAKE3 hex digest, not raw bytes) is what the broker will sign over — migration is custody-only, not surface. 18 new tests bring workspace total from 158 → 176. cargo build/test/clippy/fmt all PASS.

T-010: Segment sealing + cross-segment linkage per S3.1 §5.2 + §7. Two new modules in aios-evidence: segment.rs (1022 LoC) and segment_chain.rs (415 LoC). SegmentId is `seg_<32hex>` content-addressed (BLAKE3-truncated, no random new()) following the TransitionPlanId pattern — colon-form rejected, upper-case rejected, wrong-prefix rejected. Segment::new(retention_class) opens an empty segment; Segment::append(receipt) is the only mutator and short-circuits on already-sealed (defensive — seal consumes self). Segment::seal(prev_id, prev_seal_hash, &SigningKey) -> SealedSegment performs: (1) reject empty; (2) compute BLAKE3 of JCS over receipts-pre-terminal; (3) mint segment_id via BLAKE3(genesis_receipt_id + RFC3339 sealed_at); (4) append terminal SEGMENT_SEALED receipt (RecordType::SegmentSealed, RetentionClass::Forever, subject _system:service:evidence-segment-signer) per-receipt-signed by the same key; (5) BLAKE3 hex over canonical SegmentSealMetadata; (6) Ed25519-sign the seal-hash hex string bytes. SealedSegment has all fields private, only &self accessors, no &mut self method (INV-005 at segment boundary). verify_seal(vk) recomputes seal hash and Ed25519-verifies; verify_full(vk) walks per-receipt signatures + verify_seal. SegmentChain is the append-only cross-segment list; verify_chain checks previous_segment_seal_hash + previous_segment_id linkage; verify_chain_signed adds segment-seal verification; verify_full adds per-receipt verification. 5 new EvidenceError variants: SegmentAlreadySealed, EmptySegment, SegmentChainBroken{index,actual,expected}, SegmentSealMismatch{expected,computed}, SegmentSignatureMismatch. 28 new tests (24 unit in segment.rs + segment_chain.rs + 2 integration in tests/segment_lifecycle.rs) bring workspace total from 176 → 204. cargo build/test/clippy --all-targets -D warnings/fmt --check all PASS.
discovered_constraints: Rust 1.94 toolchain available
Workspace lints set: unsafe_code forbid, unwrap/expect/panic deny, clippy::pedantic warn
§21 stack lock: serde + tokio + tonic + serde + tracing pinned at workspace level
chrono added to workspace deps (needed by S0.1 §5.1 Condition.last_transition_time and Execution timestamps)
thiserror auto-source: a field named `source: String` collides with the AsDynError trait — use `detail: String` for stringified non-error contexts
Tests need `#[allow(clippy::expect_used)]` since workspace denies expect/unwrap globally — applied at test mod level

serde_json::Map defaults to BTreeMap (preserve_order feature disabled at workspace level), giving lex-sorted keys for free — but we still rebuild maps explicitly during canonicalization to defend against future workspace config drift

serde_jcs 0.1.0 (Governor-approved T-006) is the canonical Rust RFC 8785 implementation; pulls in ryu-js for ECMA-262 number normalization; ECMA-262 number normalization deficit from T-002 is now CLOSED — numerically-equal floats produce identical canonical bytes
clippy::too_long_first_doc_paragraph fires when a macro-emitted doc comment's first paragraph spans multiple sentences; split into one-sentence summary + blank doc line + body
clippy::many_single_char_names fires at 5+ single-char locals in one scope — use descriptive names
TransitionPlanId has NO random new() constructor by design — content addressing is the invariant (S15.2 §5.3 invariant 5: byte-identical replay); adding new() would silently violate this
clippy::doc_markdown flags bare PascalCase identifiers in docs — wrap policy/condition vocabulary words in backticks; even casual mentions like `serde_json` in doc comments must be backticked
clippy::panic denies panic! in non-test code; for tests, extend the test mod's #[allow(...)] to include clippy::panic when destructuring assertions need a panic-on-mismatch arm
Execution::pending() was `const fn` in T-001 but had to drop `const` in T-004 because chrono::Utc::now() is not const — initial phase_changed_at stamp must be a wall-clock now
clippy::match_same_arms fires when arms with the same body are not consolidated; when the spec's §7.3 grouping is load-bearing for readability, scope an #[allow(...)] to the single function
clippy::redundant_clone in tests requires either consuming the value (`let _ = x.into()`) or capturing what you need *before* the consuming conversion
Inside a struct impl, `Option<Box<Self>>` is preferred over `Option<Box<SelfTypeName>>` (clippy::use_self)
clippy::type_complexity fires on `[(fn(&mut T), U); N]` array types in tests — declare a local `type FnAlias = fn(&mut T);` and use that to keep the test fixture readable

Integration tests use `tests/<name>.rs` and a shared `tests/common/mod.rs` (no Cargo.toml entry needed for sub-modules under tests/); each test target compiles with `mod common;` and the workspace fully respects `cargo test --test <name>`
Integration test fixtures live under `tests/fixtures/<name>.json`; resolve via `env!("CARGO_MANIFEST_DIR").join("tests/fixtures")` rather than relying on cwd

ActionId etc are `#[serde(transparent)]` String newtypes — JSON deserialize does NOT run the ulid validator (validation only happens via explicit `parse(&str)`); fixtures may carry any opaque string in id-typed fields and still round-trip lossless

T-007: Type-level append-only invariant (INV-005) requires (1) ALL receipt fields private, (2) ONLY &self accessors — no &mut self anywhere, (3) construction routed exclusively through a consuming Builder.seal(self) that returns the immutable value, (4) a Sealed marker trait with a private super-trait to prevent downstream impl. Deserialize is still derive'd so receipts can be loaded from disk, but the chain's append/verify_integrity re-validates link hashes — no edit-and-re-serialize path exists in the public API.

T-007: clippy::doc_markdown fires on EVERY bare PascalCase identifier and on SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE words like 'RecordType' / 'PascalCase' / 'SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE' / 'RocksDB' in doc comments — wrap each in backticks at write time to avoid lint-fix loops.

T-007: `Vec::len`/`Vec::is_empty` are const since Rust 1.39 — clippy::missing_const_for_fn fires if a no-mut method wrapping them is not const fn. Mark all read-only chain accessors `const fn` where the body is const-eligible.
T-007: assert!(str.contains('x')) with a 1-char needle MUST use a char literal not a string — clippy::single_char_pattern enforces it.

T-007: integration tests that build long fixture chains routinely exceed clippy::too_many_lines (default 100). Apply `#![allow(clippy::too_many_lines, reason = "fixture builders")]` at the test file's crate-level attribute block.
discovered_dependencies: chrono 0.4 (std + clock + serde features, default-features = false)
serde_jcs 0.1 (Governor approved T-006 — RFC 8785 canonical JSON for request_hash + idempotency_hash; pulls in ryu-js for ECMA-262 number formatting)

aios-evidence depends on aios-action via path = "../aios-action" — first cross-crate workspace dependency, exercises the public re-exports surface (ActionId / EvidenceReceiptId / jcs_canonicalize / blake3_hash / blake3_truncated / CanonicalError / IdError)

ed25519-dalek 2.1 (BSD-3-Clause, dalek-cryptography) — features `std` (default; covers alloc + sha2 std) and `rand_core` (exposes keygen for tests). Production keys come from S5.2 Vault Broker for subject _system:service:evidence-segment-signer; the in-crate SigningKey path is a foundation only. SigningKey::from_bytes(&[u8; 32]) and signing_key.sign(msg) (via Signer trait) and verifying_key.verify(msg, &sig) (via Verifier trait). 64-byte Signature, 32-byte SigningKey/VerifyingKey. License BSD-3-Clause is compatible with workspace Apache-2.0 (permissive, attribution-only) — corrected from T-009 task brief's MIT/Apache-2.0 claim.
rand_core 0.6 with `getrandom` backend — tests only; never used in production crate code (no top-level rand-based code paths in aios-evidence).
last_verification_result: T-010 complete: cargo build --workspace PASS; cargo test --workspace PASS (204 tests: 76 aios-action unit + 12+8+6 aios-action integration (26) + 97 aios-evidence unit + 3 lifecycle_chain integration + 2 segment_lifecycle integration = 204 total, +28 over T-009's 176); cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings PASS; cargo fmt --check PASS
